question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getCommon() / pathPrefix + bucketName] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the string concatenation in getPrefix() where pathPrefix is prepended without sanitization, injecting path traversal sequences (e.g., '../') into pathPrefix to redirect S3 key lookups to a different bucket prefix containing attacker-controlled stream files with a reorganized transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a very short frequency value (e.g., 1ms) combined with a high-latency stream source to cause the downloader to issue overlapping poll cycles, resulting in duplicate or out-of-order processing of stream files and a reorganized transaction history in the mirror node's database?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getCommon() / maxSize] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the maxSize check in S3StreamFileProvider where size is derived from the Content-Range header rather than actual content length, by serving a response with a spoofed Content-Range header that understates the file size, causing an oversized malicious stream file to bypass the size check and be ingested as valid transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: isWriteFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the archiveFile() call triggered by isWriteFiles() to cause a path traversal by injecting special characters into the stream filename, writing attacker-controlled content to an arbitrary filesystem location that is later read as authoritative transaction history by another component?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getCommon() / consensusRatio boundary] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the RoundingMode.CEILING in getStakeRequiredForConsensus() combined with a very small consensusRatio (e.g., 0.001) to reduce the stake threshold to 1 unit, making it trivial for a single low-stake node to satisfy consensus and accept a reorganized transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getStreamType() / verifyHashChain()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the verifyHashChain() bypass when SHA_384.isHashEmpty(expectedPreviousHash) returns true (i.e., when the previous hash is all zeros or null) by deleting or corrupting the last processed stream file record, causing the downloader to accept any stream file as the next in sequence and reorganize transaction history from that point forward?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getCommon() / pathType NODE_ID] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the NODE_ID path format (network/shard/nodeId/suffix/) by registering a cloud storage path that matches a valid node ID but serves attacker-controlled stream files, causing the downloader in NODE_ID mode to fetch and ingest a reorganized transaction history from the attacker's path?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: isEnabled() / shouldDownload()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a TOCTOU race between shouldDownload() returning true and the actual download execution by injecting a burst of attacker-controlled files into the stream source during the window, causing the downloader to process attacker files before legitimate ones and insert them into transaction history out of order?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getCommon() / batchSize] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the batchSize limit in downloadAndParseSigFiles() by pre-populating the stream source with exactly batchSize attacker-controlled signature files that sort lexically before the legitimate next file, causing the entire batch to be consumed by attacker files and the legitimate transaction to be deferred indefinitely?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getCommon() / timeout + Flux.timeout()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the reactive Flux.timeout() in downloadAndParseSigFiles() by causing exactly one node's response to arrive just after the timeout, resulting in an incomplete signature set that either fails consensus or passes with fewer nodes, allowing a minority of attacker-controlled nodes to determine which transaction history is accepted?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getStreamType() / StreamFilename ordering] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lexicographic ordering of StreamFilename in the TreeMultimap used in getStreamFileSignatureMultiMap() by crafting filenames that sort before legitimate files but represent future timestamps, causing the downloader to process attacker files first and establish them as the canonical transaction history before legitimate files arrive?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getCommon() / sources + CompositeStreamFileProvider] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the CompositeStreamFileProvider's fallback behavior across multiple sources by making the primary source return empty results, forcing the downloader to fall back to a secondary source that the attacker controls, serving a reorganized transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: isEnabled() / setEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of synchronization around setEnabled() in a multi-threaded environment where multiple download threads share the same DownloaderProperties instance, causing a race condition where one thread disables the downloader while another is mid-batch, resulting in partial transaction history being committed and the remainder skipped?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getCommon() / region] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the region configuration in StreamSourceProperties to redirect S3 requests to a different AWS region where the attacker has created a bucket with the same name, serving attacker-controlled stream files and reorganizing the mirror node's transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getCommon() / pathType AUTO + pathRefreshInterval] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the AUTO path type's timed refresh by ensuring the legitimate NODE_ID path returns empty results during the refresh window, causing the downloader to revert to ACCOUNT_ID path and fetch from an attacker-controlled ACCOUNT_ID-format path, ingesting a reorganized transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getCommon() / downloadRatio + partialCollection() stake calculation] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the stake-based node selection in partialCollection() where getTotalStake() is read from the first node in the shuffled list, by manipulating the node stake data to make a low-stake attacker-controlled node appear to have high stake, causing it to be selected more often and its transaction history to dominate?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: isWriteSignatures() / archiveFile()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the writeSignatures path to cause signature files to be written to a world-readable directory, then read those signatures to understand the hash values of legitimate stream files and craft replacement files with matching hashes (via collision attack) to reorganize transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getCommon() / maxSize + S3 range request] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the S3 range request (bytes=0 to maxSize-1) in S3StreamFileProvider.get() to cause the downloader to only read the first maxSize bytes of a larger stream file, truncating transaction records at the end of the file and creating an incomplete transaction history entry that passes hash verification on the truncated content?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getStreamType() / verifyHashChain() chained check] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the verifyHashChain() early return when streamFile.getType().isChained() returns false, by uploading a stream file of a non-chained type (e.g., BALANCE) in place of a chained type (e.g., RECORD), bypassing the hash chain verification entirely and inserting arbitrary transaction history without continuity checks?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getCommon() / consensusRatio + stake=0 edge case] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the SignatureVerificationException thrown when totalStake is 0 in getStakeRequiredForConsensus(), by manipulating the node stake data to report 0 total stake, causing all consensus validation to fail and the downloader to halt, effectively freezing transaction history at an attacker-chosen point?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getFrequency() / downloadNextBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the frequency-based scheduling to time the injection of attacker-controlled stream files to arrive in the stream source exactly between two legitimate poll cycles, ensuring the attacker's file is the first result in the next batch and is processed before the legitimate file, inserting it into transaction history out of order?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getCommon() / pathType ACCOUNT_ID] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the ACCOUNT_ID path format (streamType/nodePrefix+accountId/) by registering a Hedera account with an ID that matches a legitimate node's account ID format, then uploading stream files to the corresponding bucket path, causing the downloader to ingest attacker-controlled transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: isEnabled() / setEnabled() + endDate check] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the endDate disable logic in verifySignatures() where setEnabled(false) is called when a file's instant is after endDate, by injecting a stream file with a timestamp just after endDate into the stream source, causing the downloader to disable itself and omit all legitimate transaction history that follows?
